title: Wait for Elements
description: Master Playwright's auto-waiting and explicit waiting strategies for reliable tests
instructions:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Waits for page to be fully loaded
2. Waits for specific elements to appear
3. Waits for elements to be visible
4. Waits for network to be idle
5. Uses custom timeout values

starterCode.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_wait_elements(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/dynamic-loading/1')
    
    # TODO: Click the start button
    
    # TODO: Wait for the loading spinner to disappear
    
    # TODO: Wait for the result text to appear
    
    # TODO: Assert the result text is visible
    pass

solution.python:
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect

def test_wait_elements(page: Page):
    page.goto('https://practice.expandtesting.com/dynamic-loading/1')
    
    page.click('button:has-text("Start")')
    
    page.wait_for_selector('#loading', state='hidden')
    
    page.wait_for_selector('#finish', state='visible')
    
    expect(page.locator('#finish')).to_contain_text('Hello World!')

hints: Use waitForSelector with state: 'hidden' to wait for elements to disappear
Use waitForSelector with state: 'visible' to wait for elements to appear
Playwright auto-waits for most actions, but explicit waits are useful for dynamic content
Use page.waitForLoadState() to wait for page load states
Set timeout option for custom wait durations

testCases.name: Loading spinner disappears
testCases.expectedBehavior: Wait for loading spinner to be hidden
